meta.title: Gerard Cabot Agustín — Portfolio
meta.description: Data scientist and software engineer specialising in predictive modelling and football recruiting analytics — based in Barcelona and open to relocation.

hero.roles: Data Scientist · Software Engineer · Football Analytics
hero.location: Based in Barcelona · Open to relocation
hero.sub: Modelling, platforms, and the occasional messy Postgres query — usually for scouts and fans behind the scenes, or for the next product release shipping under load.
hero.focusPlain: Football DS research collaborator at Levante UD; durable Python ingestion and Postgres work at Fever for product-scale workloads.

about.heading: About
about.p1: I am a data scientist and software engineer focused on predictive modelling and recruiting analytics in football. I care about artefacts that survive hand-off: reproducible notebooks, observable services, dashboards that scouts revisit without a fresh onboarding every Monday.
about.p2Lead: Most of my workload sits between
about.p2Trail: , where I ship Python services and ingestion at sustained traffic — Postgres tuning included.
about.challengeLead: Outside the codebase: second place at the
about.bookLead: and credited participation alongside Levante in Alejandro Losa Brito's
about.bookAside: Scout-facing book on imperceptible detail in modern football — paperback in Spanish (
about.eduHonoursLead: — thesis graded Excellent with Honours; same lineage as

bullets: Similar Brain research: comparing players through passing intent (what they chose on the pitch) rather than blunt completion percentages, powering likeness workflows for recruitment cycles.
Packaging similarity outputs so scouts revisit them: exploratory shortlist profiles alongside post-match reviews that tighten tactical narratives.
Own durable Python services and ingestion paths that back event-discovery experiences under real product load.
Refactor SQL-heavy Postgres paths—profile hot queries and reshape schemas or batch boundaries so near-real-time flows stay predictable during spikes.
Deliver bespoke ABAP objects inside large footprints—custom logic, integrations, extensions, and succinct hand-off documentation for downstream squads.
Work with architects and analysts to unblock cross-module flows without ballooning regression risk.
Led roster development, scouting, and substitution decisions across federated age-group programmes.
Built nimble possession and shot-tracking sheets so scouting notes rested on repeatable KPIs, not anecdotes.

skills.heading: Skills & tooling
skills.intro: Seven buckets that mirror how I recruit in CV conversations: coding languages first, quantitative foundations, modelling, infra, interfaces, football-specific tooling, then production footprints.

projects.heading: Projects
projects.intro: Two artefacts recruiters still hear about—one shipped as honours thesis coursework, another from a metropolitan smart-meter anomaly challenge.

cards.title: Stars of the Future
cards.description: Automated scouting stack from my UPF thesis (Excellent with Honours): StatsBomb KPIs, XGBoost for young LaLiga prospects, Flask + React services, deployed for live comparisons.

cards.title: Aigües de Barcelona Data Challenge
cards.description: Anomaly detection on telelectura smart-meter footprints across Barcelona’s metropolitan water cohort.

contact.heading: Contact
contact.intro: For hiring scopes, scouting analytics partnerships, or engineering conversations — email is quickest. I respond in Catalan, Spanish, or English in the voice you wrote with.
